      data: DOMString,\n        can_gc: CanGc,\n    ) -> Fallible<DomRoot<CDATASection>> {\n        // Step 1\n        if self.is_html_document {\n            return Err(Error::NotSupported(None));\n        }\n\n        // Step 2\n        if data.contains(\"]]>\") {\n            return Err(Error::InvalidCharacter(None));\n        }\n\n        // Step 3\n        Ok(CDATASection::new(data, self, can_gc))\n    }\n\n    /// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#dom-document-createcomment>\n    fn CreateComment(&self, data: DOMString, can_gc: CanGc) -> DomRoot<Comment> {\n        Comment::new(data, self, None, can_gc)\n    }\n\n    /// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#dom-document-createprocessinginstruction>\n    fn CreateProcessingInstruction(\n        &self,\n        target: DOMString,\n        data: DOMString,\n        can_gc: CanGc,\n    ) -> Fallible<DomRoot<ProcessingInstruction>> {\n        // Step 1. If target does not match the Name production, then throw an \"InvalidCharacterError\" DOMException.\n        if !matches_name_production(&target.str()) {\n            return Err(Error::InvalidCharacter(None));\n        }\n\n        // Step 2.\n        if data.contains(\"?>\") {\n            return Err(Error::InvalidCharacter(None));\n        }\n\n        // Step 3.\n        Ok(ProcessingInstruction::new(target, data, self, can_gc))\n    }\n\n    /// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#dom-document-importnode>\n    fn ImportNode(\n        &self,\n        node: &Node,\n        options: BooleanOrImportNodeOptions,\n        can_gc: CanGc,\n    ) -> Fallible<DomRoot<Node>> {\n        // Step 1. If node is a document or shadow root, then throw a \"NotSupportedError\" DOMException.\n        if node.is::<Document>() || node.is::<ShadowRoot>() {\n            return Err(Error::NotSupported(None));\n        }\n        // Step 2. Let subtree be false.\n        let (subtree, registry) = match options {\n            // Step 3. Let registry be null.\n            // Step 4. If options is a boolean, then set subtree to options.\n            BooleanOrImportNodeOptions::Boolean(boolean) => (boolean.into(), None),\n            // Step 5. Otherwise:\n            BooleanOrImportNodeOptions::ImportNodeOptions(options) => {\n                // Step 5.1. Set subtree to the negation of options[\"selfOnly\"].\n                let subtree = (!options.selfOnly).into();\n                // Step 5.2. If options[\"customElementRegistry\"] exists, then set registry to it.\n                let registry = options.customElementRegistry;\n                // Step 5.3. If registry’s is scoped is false and registry\n                // is not this’s custom element registry, then throw a \"NotSupportedError\" DOMException.\n                // TODO\n                (subtree, registry)\n            },\n        };\n        // Step 6. If registry is null, then set registry to the\n        // result of looking up a custom element registry given this.\n        let registry = registry\n            .or_else(|| CustomElementRegistry::lookup_a_custom_element_registry(self.upcast()));\n\n        // Step 7. Return the result of cloning a node given node with\n        // document set to this, subtree set to subtree, and fallbackRegistry set to registry.\n        Ok(Node::clone(node, Some(self), subtree, registry, can_gc))\n    }\n\n    /// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#dom-document-adoptnode>\n    fn AdoptNode(&self, node: &Node, can_gc: CanGc) -> Fallible<DomRoot<Node>> {\n        // Step 1.\n        if node.is::<Document>() {\n            return Err(Error::NotSupported(None));\n        }\n\n        // Step 2.\n        if node.is::<ShadowRoot>() {\n            return Err(Error::HierarchyRequest(None));\n        }\n\n        // Step 3.\n        Node::adopt(node, self, can_gc);\n\n        // Step 4.\n        Ok(DomRoot::from_ref(node))\n    }\n\n    /// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#dom-document-createevent>\n    fn CreateEvent(&self, mut interface: DOMString, can_gc: CanGc) -> Fallible<DomRoot<Event>> {\n        interface.make_ascii_lowercase();\n        match &*interface.str() {\n            \"beforeunloadevent\" => Ok(DomRoot::upcast(BeforeUnloadEvent::new_uninitialized(\n                &self.window,\n                can_gc,\n            ))),\n            \"compositionevent\" | \"textevent\" => Ok(DomRoot::upcast(\n                CompositionEvent::new_uninitialized(&self.window, can_gc),\n            )),\n            \"customevent\" => Ok(DomRoot::upcast(CustomEvent::new_uninitialized(\n                self.window.upcast(),\n                can_gc,\n            ))),\n            // FIXME(#25136): devicemotionevent, deviceorientationevent\n            // FIXME(#7529): dragevent\n            \"events\" | \"event\" | \"htmlevents\" | \"svgevents\" => {\n                Ok(Event::new_uninitialized(self.window.upcast(), can_gc))\n            },\n            \"focusevent\" => Ok(DomRoot::upcast(FocusEvent::new_uninitialized(\n                &self.window,\n                can_gc,\n            ))),\n            \"hashchangeevent\" => Ok(DomRoot::upcast(HashChangeEvent::new_uninitialized(\n                &self.window,\n                can_gc,\n            ))),\n            \"keyboardevent\" => Ok(DomRoot::upcast(KeyboardEvent::new_uninitialized(\n                &self.window,\n                can_gc,\n            ))),\n            \"messageevent\" => Ok(DomRoot::upcast(MessageEvent::new_uninitialized(\n                self.window.upcast(),\n                can_gc,\n            ))),\n            \"mouseevent\" | \"mouseevents\" => Ok(DomRoot::upcast(MouseEvent::new_uninitialized(\n                &self.window,\n                can_gc,\n            ))),\n            \"storageevent\" => Ok(DomRoot::upcast(StorageEvent::new_uninitialized(\n                &self.window,\n                \"\".into(),\n                can_gc,\n            ))),\n            \"touchevent\" => Ok(DomRoot::upcast(DomTouchEvent::new_uninitialized(\n                &self.window,\n                &TouchList::new(&self.window, &[], can_gc),\n                &TouchList::new(&self.window, &[], can_gc),\n                &TouchList::new(&self.window, &[], can_gc),\n                can_gc,\n            ))),\n            \"uievent\" | \"uievents\" => Ok(DomRoot::upcast(UIEvent::new_uninitialized(\n                &self.window,\n                can_gc,\n            ))),\n            _ => Err(Error::NotSupported(None)),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#dom-document-lastmodified>\n    fn LastModified(&self) -> DOMString {\n        DOMString::from(self.last_modified.as_ref().cloned().unwrap_or_else(|| {\n            // Ideally this would get the local time using `time`, but `time` always fails to get the local\n            // timezone on Unix unless the application is single threaded unless the library is explicitly\n            // set to \"unsound\" mode. Maybe that's fine, but it needs more investigation. see\n            // https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2020-26235\n            // When `time` supports a thread-safe way of getting the local time zone we could use it here.\n            Local::now().format(\"%m/%d/%Y %H:%M:%S\").to_string()\n        }))\n    }\n\n    /// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#dom-document-createrange>\n    fn <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#dom-document-createnodeiteratorroot-whattoshow-filter>\n    fn CreateNodeIterator(\n        &self,\n        root: &Node,\n        what_to_show: u32,\n        filter: <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#dom-document-createtreewalker>\n    fn CreateTreeWalker(\n        &self,\n        root: &Node,\n        what_to_show: u32,\n        filter: Option<Rc<NodeFilter>>,\n    ) -> DomRoot<TreeWalker> {\n        TreeWalker::new(self, root, what_to_show, filter)\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#document.title>\n    fn Title(&self) -> DOMString {\n        self.title().unwrap_or_else(|| DOMString::from(\"\"))\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#document.title>\n    fn SetTitle(&self, title: DOMString, can_gc: CanGc) {\n        let root = match self.GetDocumentElement() {\n            Some(root) => root,\n            None => return,\n        };\n\n        let node = if root.namespace() == &ns!(svg) && root.local_name() == &local_name!(\"svg\") {\n            let elem = root.upcast::<Node>().child_elements().find(|node| {\n                node.namespace() == &ns!(svg) && node.local_name() == &local_name!(\"title\")\n            });\n            match elem {\n                Some(elem) => DomRoot::upcast::<Node>(elem),\n                None => {\n                    let name = QualName::new(None, ns!(svg), local_name!(\"title\"));\n                    let elem = Element::create(\n                        name,\n                        None,\n                        self,\n                        ElementCreator::ScriptCreated,\n                        CustomElementCreationMode::Synchronous,\n                        None,\n                        can_gc,\n                    );\n                    let parent = root.upcast::<Node>();\n                    let child = elem.upcast::<Node>();\n                    parent\n                        .InsertBefore(child, parent.GetFirstChild().as_deref(), can_gc)\n                        .unwrap()\n                },\n            }\n        } else if root.namespace() == &ns!(html) {\n            let elem = root\n                .upcast::<Node>()\n                .traverse_preorder(ShadowIncluding::No)\n                .find(|node| node.is::<HTMLTitleElement>());\n            match elem {\n                Some(elem) => elem,\n                None => match self.GetHead() {\n                    Some(head) => {\n                        let name = QualName::new(None, ns!(html), local_name!(\"title\"));\n                        let elem = Element::create(\n                            name,\n                            None,\n                            self,\n                            ElementCreator::ScriptCreated,\n                            CustomElementCreationMode::Synchronous,\n                            None,\n                            can_gc,\n                        );\n                        head.upcast::<Node>()\n                            .AppendChild(elem.upcast(), can_gc)\n                            .unwrap()\n                    },\n                    None => return,\n                },\n            }\n        } else {\n            return;\n        };\n\n        node.set_text_content_for_element(Some(title), can_gc);\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#dom-document-head>\n    fn GetHead(&self) -> Option<DomRoot<HTMLHeadElement>> {\n        self.get_html_element()\n            .and_then(|root| root.upcast::<Node>().children().find_map(DomRoot::downcast))\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#dom-document-currentscript>\n    fn GetCurrentScript(&self) -> Option<DomRoot<HTMLScriptElement>> {\n        self.current_script.get()\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#dom-document-body>\n    fn GetBody(&self) -> Option<DomRoot<HTMLElement>> {\n        self.get_html_element().and_then(|root| {\n            let node = root.upcast::<Node>();\n            node.children()\n                .find(|child| {\n                    matches!(\n                        child.type_id(),\n                        NodeTypeId::Element(ElementTypeId::HTMLElement(\n                            HTMLElementTypeId::HTMLBodyElement,\n                        )) | NodeTypeId::Element(ElementTypeId::HTMLElement(\n                            HTMLElementTypeId::HTMLFrameSetElement,\n                        ))\n                    )\n                })\n                .map(|node| DomRoot::downcast(node).unwrap())\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#dom-document-body>\n    fn SetBody(&self, new_body: Option<&HTMLElement>, can_gc: CanGc) -> ErrorResult {\n        // Step 1.\n        let new_body = match new_body {\n            Some(new_body) => new_body,\n            None => return Err(Error::HierarchyRequest(None)),\n        };\n\n        let node = new_body.upcast::<Node>();\n        match node.type_id() {\n            NodeTypeId::Element(ElementTypeId::HTMLElement(HTMLElementTypeId::HTMLBodyElement)) |\n            NodeTypeId::Element(ElementTypeId::HTMLElement(\n                HTMLElementTypeId::HTMLFrameSetElement,\n            )) => {},\n            _ => return Err(Error::HierarchyRequest(None)),\n        }\n\n        // Step 2.\n        let old_body = self.GetBody();\n        if old_body.as_deref() == Some(new_body) {\n            return Ok(());\n        }\n\n        match (self.GetDocumentElement(), &old_body) {\n            // Step 3.\n            (Some(ref root), Some(child)) => {\n                let root = root.upcast::<Node>();\n                root.ReplaceChild(new_body.upcast(), child.upcast(), can_gc)\n                    .unwrap();\n            },\n\n            // Step 4.\n            (None, _) => return Err(Error::HierarchyRequest(None)),\n\n            // Step 5.\n            (Some(ref root), &None) => {\n                let root = root.upcast::<Node>();\n                root.AppendChild(new_body.upcast(), can_gc).unwrap();\n            },\n        }\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#dom-document-getelementsbyname>\n    fn GetElementsByName(&self, name: DOMString, can_gc: CanGc) -> DomRoot<NodeList> {\n        NodeList::new_elements_by_name_list(self.window(), self, name, can_gc)\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#dom-document-images>\n    fn Images(&self, can_gc: CanGc) -> _| element.is::<HTMLImageElement>(),\n                can_gc,\n            )\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#dom-document-embeds>\n    fn Embeds(&self, can_gc:         )\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#dom-document-applets>\n    fn Applets(&self, can_gc: CanGc) -> DomRoot<HTMLCollection> {\n        self.applets\n            .or_init(|| HTMLCollection::always_empty(&self.window, self.upcast(), can_gc))\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#dom-document-location>\n    fn GetLocation(&self) -> <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#dom-parentnode-children>\n    fn Children(&self, can_gc: CanGc) -> DomRoot<HTMLCollection> {\n        HTMLCollection::children(&self.window, self.upcast(), can_gc)\n    }\n\n    /// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#dom-parentnode-firstelementchild>\n    fn GetFirstElementChild(&self) -> Option<DomRoot<Element>> {\n        self.upcast::<Node>().child_elements().next()\n    }\n\n    /// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#dom-parentnode-lastelementchild>\n    fn GetLastElementChild(&self) -> Option<DomRoot<Element>> {\n        self.upcast::<Node>()\n            .rev_children()\n            .find_map(DomRoot::downcast)\n    }\n\n    /// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#dom-parentnode-childelementcount>\n    fn ChildElementCount(&self) -> u32 {\n Vec<NodeOrString>, can_gc: CanGc) -> ErrorResult {\n        self.upcast::<Node>().replace_children(nodes, can_gc)\n    }\n\n    /// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#dom-parentnode-queryselector>\n    fn <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#dom-parentnode-queryselectorall>\n    fn QuerySelectorAll(&self, selectors: DOMString) -> Fallible<DomRoot<NodeList>> {\n        let root = self.upcast::<Node>();\n        root.query_selector_all(selectors)\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#dom-document-readystate>\n    fn ReadyState(&self) -> DocumentReadyState {\n        self.ready_state.get()\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#dom-document-defaultview>\n    fn GetDefaultView(&self) -> Option<DomRoot<Window>> {\n        if self.has_browsing_context {\n            Some(DomRoot::from_ref(&*self.window))\n        } else {\n            None\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#dom-document-cookie>\n    fn GetCookie(&self) -> Fallible<DOMString> {\n        if self.is_cookie_averse() {\n            return Ok(DOMString::new());\n        }\n\n        if !self.origin.is_tuple() {\n            return Err(Error::Security(None));\n        }\n\n        let url = self.url();\n        let (tx, rx) = profile_ipc::channel(self.global().time_profiler_chan().clone()).unwrap();\n        let _ = self\n            .window\n            .as_global_scope()\n            .resource_threads()\n            .send(GetCookiesForUrl(url, tx, NonHTTP));\n        let cookies = rx.recv().unwrap();\n        Ok(cookies.map_or(DOMString::new(), DOMString::from))\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#dom-document-cookie>\n    fn SetCookie(&self, cookie: DOMString) -> ErrorResult {\n        if self.is_cookie_averse() {\n            return Ok(());\n        }\n\n        if !self.origin.is_tuple() {\n            return Err(Error::Security(None));\n        }\n\n        if !cookie.is_valid_for_cookie() {\n            return Ok(());\n        }\n\n        let cookies = if let Some(cookie) = Cookie::parse(cookie.to_string()).ok().map(Serde) {\n            vec![cookie]\n        } else {\n            vec![]\n        };\n\n        let _ = self\n            .window\n            .as_global_scope()\n            .resource_threads()\n            .send(SetCookiesForUrl(self.url(), cookies, NonHTTP));\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#dom-document-bgcolor>\n    fn BgColor(&self) -> DOMString {\n        self.get_body_attribute(&local_name!(\"bgcolor\"))\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#dom-document-bgcolor>\n    fn SetBgColor(&self, value: DOMString, can_gc: CanGc) {\n        self.set_body_attribute(&local_name!(\"bgcolor\"), value, can_gc)\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#dom-document-fgcolor>\n    fn FgColor(&self) -> DOMString {\n        self.get_body_attribute(&local_name!(\"text\"))\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#dom-document-fgcolor>\n    fn SetFgColor(&self, value: DOMString, can_gc: CanGc) {\n        self.set_body_attribute(&local_name!(\"text\"), value, can_gc)\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#dom-tree-accessors:dom-document-nameditem-filter>\n    fn NamedGetter(&self, name: DOMString, can_gc: CanGc) -> Option<NamedPropertyValue> {\n        if name.is_empty() {\n            return None;\n        }\n        let name = Atom::from(name);\n\n        // Step 1. Let elements be the list of named elements with the name name that are in a document tree\n        // with the Document as their root.\n        let elements_with_name = self.get_elements_with_name(&name);\n        let name_iter = elements_with_name\n            .iter()\n            .filter(|elem| is_named_element_with_name_attribute(elem));\n        let elements_with_id = self.get_elements_with_id(&name);\n        let id_iter = elements_with_id\n            .iter()\n            .filter(|elem| is_named_element_with_id_attribute(elem));\n        let mut elements = name_iter.chain(id_iter);\n\n        // Step 2. If elements has only one element, and that element is an iframe element,\n        // and that iframe element's content navigable is not null, then return the active\n        // WindowProxy of the element's content navigable.\n\n        // NOTE: We have to check if all remaining elements are equal to the first, since\n        // the same element may appear in both lists.\n        let first = elements.next()?;\n        if elements.all(|other| first == other) {\n            if let Some(nested_window_proxy) = first\n                .downcast::<HTMLIFrameElement>()\n                .and_then(|iframe| iframe.GetContentWindow())\n            {\n                return Some(NamedPropertyValue::WindowProxy(nested_window_proxy));\n            }\n\n            // Step 3. Otherwise, if elements has only one element, return that element.\n            return Some(NamedPropertyValue::Element(DomRoot::from_ref(first)));\n        }\n\n        // Step 4. Otherwise, return an HTMLCollection rooted at the Document node,\n        // whose filter matches only named elements with the name name.\n        #[derive(JSTraceable, MallocSizeOf)]\n        struct DocumentNamedGetter {\n            #[no_trace]\n            name: Atom,\n        }\n        impl CollectionFilter for DocumentNamedGetter {\n            fn filter(&self, elem: &Element, _root: &Node) -> bool {\n                let type_ = match elem.upcast::<Node>().type_id() {\n                    NodeTypeId::Element(ElementTypeId::HTMLElement(type_)) => type_,\n                    _ => return false,\n                };\n                match type_ {\n                    HTMLElementTypeId::HTMLFormElement | HTMLElementTypeId::HTMLIFrameElement => {\n                        elem.get_name().as_ref() == Some(&self.name)\n                    },\n                    HTMLElementTypeId::HTMLImageElement => elem.get_name().is_some_and(|name| {\n                        name == *self.name ||\n                            !name.is_empty() && elem.get_id().as_ref() == Some(&self.name)\n                    }),\n                    // TODO handle <embed> and <object>; these depend on whether the element is\n                    // “exposed”, a concept that doesn’t fully make sense until embed/object\n                    // behaviour is actually implemented\n                    _ => false,\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        let collection = HTMLCollection::create(\n            self.window(),\n            self.upcast(),\n            Box::new(DocumentNamedGetter { name }),\n            can_gc,\n        );\n        Some(NamedPropertyValue::HTMLCollection(collection))\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#dom-tree-accessors:supported-property-names>\n    fn SupportedPropertyNames(&self) -> Vec<DOMString> {\n        let mut names_with_first_named_element_map: HashMap<&Atom, &Element> = HashMap::new();\n\n        let name_map = self.name_map.borrow();\n        for (name, elements) in &(name_map).0 {\n            if name.is_empty() {\n                continue;\n            }\n            let mut name_iter = elements\n                .iter()\n                .filter(|elem| is_named_element_with_name_attribute(elem));\n            if let Some(first) = name_iter.next() {\n                names_with_first_named_element_map.insert(name, first);\n            }\n        }\n        let id_map = self.id_map.borrow();\n        for (id, elements) in &(id_map).0 {\n            if id.is_empty() {\n                continue;\n            }\n            let mut id_iter = elements\n                .iter()\n                .filter(|elem| is_named_element_with_id_attribute(elem));\n            if let Some(first) = id_iter.next() {\n                match names_with_first_named_element_map.entry(id) {\n                    Vacant(entry) => drop(entry.insert(first)),\n                    Occupied(mut entry) => {\n                        if first.upcast::<Node>().is_before(entry.get().upcast()) {\n                            *entry.get_mut() = first;\n                        }\n                    },\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        let mut names_with_first_named_element_vec: Vec<(&Atom, &Element)> =\n            names_with_first_named_element_map\n                .iter()\n                .map(|(k, v)| (*k, *v))\n                .collect();\n        names_with_first_named_element_vec.sort_unstable_by(|a, b| {\n            if a.1 == b.1 {\n                // This can happen if an img has an id different from its name,\n                // spec does not say which string to put first.\n                a.0.cmp(b.0)\n            } else if a.1.upcast::<Node>().is_before(b.1.upcast::<Node>()) {\n                Ordering::Less\n            } else {\n                Ordering::Greater\n            }\n        });\n\n        names_with_first_named_element_vec\n            .iter()\n            .map(|(k, _v)| DOMString::from(&***k))\n            .collect()\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#dom-document-clear>\n    fn Clear(&self) {\n        // This method intentionally does nothing\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#dom-document-captureevents>\n    fn CaptureEvents(&self) {\n        // This method intentionally does nothing\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#dom-document-releaseevents>\n    fn ReleaseEvents(&self) {\n        // This method intentionally does nothing\n    }\n\n    // https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#globaleventhandlers\n    global_event_handlers!();\n\n    // https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#handler-onreadystatechange\n    event_handler!(\n        readystatechange,\n        GetOnreadystatechange,\n        SetOnreadystatechange\n    );\n\n    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom-view/#dom-document-elementfrompoint>\n    fn ElementFromPoint(&self, x: Finite<f64>, y: Finite<f64>) -> Option<DomRoot<Element>> {\n        self.document_or_shadow_root.element_from_point(\n            x,\n            y,\n            self.GetDocumentElement(),\n            self.has_browsing_context,\n        )\n    }\n\n    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom-view/#dom-document-elementsfrompoint>\n    fn ElementsFromPoint(&self, x: Finite<f64>, y: Finite<f64>) -> Vec<DomRoot<Element>> {\n        self.document_or_shadow_root.elements_from_point(\n            x,\n            y,\n            self.GetDocumentElement(),\n            self.has_browsing_context,\n        )\n    }\n\n    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom-view/#dom-document-scrollingelement>\n    fn GetScrollingElement(&self) -> Option<DomRoot<Element>> {\n        // Step 1. If the Document is in quirks mode, follow these steps:\n        if self.quirks_mode() == QuirksMode::Quirks {\n            // Step 1.1. If the body element exists,\n            if let Some(ref body) = self.GetBody() {\n                let e = body.upcast::<Element>();\n                // and it is not potentially scrollable, return the body element and abort these steps.\n                // For this purpose, a value of overflow:clip on the body element’s parent element\n                // must be treated as overflow:hidden.\n                if !e.is_potentially_scrollable_body_for_scrolling_element() {\n                    return Some(DomRoot::from_ref(e));\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Step 1.2. Return null and abort these steps.\n            return None;\n        }\n\n        // Step 2. If there is a root element, return the root element and abort these steps.\n        // Step 3. Return null.\n        self.GetDocumentElement()\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#dom-document-open>\n    fn Open(\n        &self,\n        _unused1: Option<DOMString>,\n        _unused2: Option<DOMString>,\n        can_gc: CanGc,\n    ) -> Fallible<DomRoot<Document>> {\n        // Step 1\n        if !self.is_html_document() {\n            return Err(Error::InvalidState(None));\n        }\n\n        // Step 2\n        if self.throw_on_dynamic_markup_insertion_counter.get() > 0 {\n            return Err(Error::InvalidState(None));\n        }\n\n        // Step 3\n        let entry_responsible_document = GlobalScope::entry().as_window().Document();\n\n        // Step 4\n        if !self.origin.same_origin(&entry_responsible_document.origin) {\n            return Err(Error::Security(None));\n        }\n\n        // Step 5\n        if self\n            .get_current_parser()\n            .is_some_and(|parser| parser.is_active())\n        {\n            return Ok(DomRoot::from_ref(self));\n        }\n\n        // Step 6\n        if self.is_prompting_or_unloading() {\n            return Ok(DomRoot::from_ref(self));\n        }\n\n        // Step 7\n        if self.active_parser_was_aborted.get() {\n            return Ok(DomRoot::from_ref(self));\n        }\n\n        // TODO: prompt to unload.\n        // TODO: set unload_event_start and unload_event_end\n\n        self.window().set_navigation_start();\n\n        // Step 8\n        // TODO: https://github.com/servo/servo/issues/21937\n        if self.has_browsing_context() {\n            // spec says \"stop document loading\",\n            // which is a process that does more than just abort\n            self.abort(can_gc);\n        }\n\n        // Step 9\n        for node in self\n            .upcast::<Node>()\n            .traverse_preorder(ShadowIncluding::Yes)\n        {\n            node.upcast::<EventTarget>().remove_all_listeners();\n        }\n\n        // Step 10\n        if self.window.Document() == DomRoot::from_ref(self) {\n            self.window.upcast::<EventTarget>().remove_all_listeners();\n        }\n\n        // Step 11. Replace all with null within document.\n        Node::replace_all(None, self.upcast::<Node>(), can_gc);\n\n        // Specs and tests are in a state of flux about whether\n        // we want to clear the selection when we remove the contents;\n        // WPT selection/Document-open.html wants us to not clear it\n        // as of Feb 1 2020\n\n        // Step 12. If document is fully active, then:\n        if self.is_fully_active() {\n            // Step 12.1. Let newURL be a copy of entryDocument's URL.\n            let mut new_url = entry_responsible_document.url();\n\n            // Step 12.2. If entryDocument is not document, then set newURL's fragment to null.\n            if entry_responsible_document != DomRoot::from_ref(self) {\n                new_url.set_fragment(None);\n            }\n\n            // Step 12.3. Run the URL and history update steps with document and newURL.\n            // TODO: https://github.com/servo/servo/issues/21939\n            self.set_url(new_url);\n        }\n\n        // Step 13. Set document's is initial about:blank to false.\n        self.is_initial_about_blank.set(false);\n\n        // Step 14. If document's iframe load in progress flag is set, then set document's mute\n        // iframe load flag.\n        // TODO: https://github.com/servo/servo/issues/21938\n\n        // Step 15: Set document to no-quirks mode.\n        self.set_quirks_mode(QuirksMode::NoQuirks);\n\n        // Step 16. Create a new HTML parser and associate it with document. This is a\n        // script-created parser (meaning that it can be closed by the document.open() and\n        // document.close() methods, and that the tokenizer will wait for an explicit call to\n        // document.close() before emitting an end-of-file token). The encoding confidence is\n        // irrelevant.\n        let resource_threads = self.window.as_global_scope().resource_threads().clone();\n        *self.loader.borrow_mut() =\n            DocumentLoader::new_with_threads(resource_threads, Some(self.url()));\n        ServoParser::parse_html_script_input(self, self.url());\n\n        // Step 17. Set the insertion point to point at just before the end of the input stream\n        // (which at this point will be empty).\n        // Handled when creating the parser in step 16\n\n        // Step 18. Update the current document readiness of document to \"loading\".\n        self.ready_state.set(DocumentReadyState::Loading);\n\n        // Step 19. Return document.\n        Ok(DomRoot::from_ref(self))\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#dom-document-open-window>\n    fn Open_(\n        &self,\n        url: USVString,\n        target: DOMString,\n        features: DOMString,\n        can_gc: CanGc,\n    ) -> Fallible<Option<DomRoot<WindowProxy>>> {\n        self.browsing_context()\n            .ok_or(Error::InvalidAccess(None))?\n            .open(url, target, features, can_gc)\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#dom-document-write>\n    fn Write(&self, text: Vec<TrustedHTMLOrString>, can_gc: CanGc) -> ErrorResult {\n        // The document.write(...text) method steps are to run the document write steps\n        // with this, text, false, and \"Document write\".\n        self.write(text, false, \"Document\", \"write\", can_gc)\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#dom-document-writeln>\n    fn Writeln(&self, text: Vec<TrustedHTMLOrString>, can_gc: CanGc) -> ErrorResult {\n        // The document.writeln(...text) method steps are to run the document write steps\n        // with this, text, true, and \"Document ErrorResult {\n        if !self.is_html_document() {\n            // Step 1.\n            return Err(Error::InvalidState(None));\n        }\n\n        // Step 2.\n        if self.throw_on_dynamic_markup_insertion_counter.get() > 0 {\n            return Err(Error::InvalidState(None));\n        }\n\n        let parser = match self.get_current_parser() {\n            Some(ref parser) if parser.is_script_created() => DomRoot::from_ref(&**parser),\n            _ => {\n                // Step 3.\n                return Ok(());\n            },\n        };\n\n        // Step 4-6.\n        parser.close(can_gc);\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    // https://fullscreen.spec.whatwg.org/#handler-document-onfullscreenerror\n    event_handler!(fullscreenerror, OneshotTimerCallback::EventSourceTimeout(callback) => callback.invoke(),\n            OneshotTimerCallback::JsTimer(task) => task.invoke(this, js_timers, can_gc),\n            #[cfg(feature = \"testbinding\")]\n            OneshotTimerCallback::TestBindingCallback(callback) => callback.invoke(),\n            OneshotTimerCallback::RefreshRedirectDue(callback) => callback.invoke(can_gc),\n            OneshotTimerCallback::RunStepsAfterTimeout { completion, .. } => {\n                // <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#run-steps-after-a-timeout>\n                // Step 4.4 Perform completionSteps.\n                completion(&this.global(), can_gc);\n            },\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Ord for OneshotTimer {\n    fn cmp(&self, other: &OneshotTimer) -> Ordering {\n        match self.scheduled_for.cmp(&other.scheduled_for).reverse() {\n            Ordering::Equal => self.handle.cmp(&other.handle).reverse(),\n            res => res,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl PartialOrd for OneshotTimer {\n    fn partial_cmp(&self, other: &OneshotTimer) -> Option<Ordering> {\n        Some(self.cmp(other))\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Eq for OneshotTimer {}\nimpl PartialEq for OneshotTimer {\n    fn eq(&self, other: &OneshotTimer) -> bool {\n        std::ptr::eq(self, other)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl OneshotTimers {\n    pub(crate) fn new(global_scope: &GlobalScope) -> OneshotTimers {\n        OneshotTimers {\n            global_scope: Dom::from_ref(global_scope),\n            js_timers: JsTimers::default(),\n            next_timer_handle: Cell::new(OneshotTimerHandle(1)),\n            timers: DomRefCell::new(VecDeque::new()),\n            suspended_since: Cell::new(None),\n            suspension_offset: Cell::new(Duration::ZERO),\n            expected_event_id: Cell::new(TimerEventId(0)),\n            map_of_active_timers: Default::default(),\n            runsteps_queues: Default::default(),\n            next_runsteps_key: Cell::new(1),\n            runsteps_start_seq: Cell::new(0),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#run-steps-after-a-timeout>\n    #[inline]\n    pub(crate) fn now_for_runsteps(&self) -> Instant {\n        // Step 2. Let startTime be the current high resolution time given global.\n        self.base_time()\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#run-steps-after-a-timeout>\n    /// Step 1. Let timerKey be a new unique internal value.\n    pub(crate) fn fresh_runsteps_key(&self) -> TimerKey {\n        let k = self.next_runsteps_key.get();\n        self.next_runsteps_key.set(k + 1);\n        k\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#run-steps-after-a-timeout>\n    /// Step 3. Set global's map of active timers[timerKey] to startTime plus milliseconds.\n    pub(crate) fn runsteps_set_active(&self, timer_key: TimerKey, deadline: RunStepsDeadline) {\n        self.map_of_active_timers\n            .borrow_mut()\n            .insert(timer_key, deadline);\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#run-steps-after-a-timeout>\n    /// Helper for Step 4.2: maintain per-ordering sorted queue by (milliseconds, startSeq, handle).\n    fn runsteps_enqueue_sorted(\n        &self,\n        ordering_id: &DOMString,\n        handle: OneshotTimerHandle,\n        milliseconds: u64,\n    ) {\n        let mut map = self.runsteps_queues.borrow_mut();\n        let q = map.entry(ordering_id.clone()).or_default();\n\n        let seq = {\n            let cur = self.runsteps_start_seq.get();\n            self.runsteps_start_seq.set(cur + 1);\n            cur\n        };\n\n        let key = OrderingEntry {\n            milliseconds,\n            start_seq: seq,\n            handle,\n        };\n\n        let idx = q\n            .binary_search_by(|ordering_entry| {\n                match ordering_entry.milliseconds.cmp(&milliseconds) {\n                    Ordering::Less => Ordering::Less,\n                    Ordering::Greater => Ordering::Greater,\n                    Ordering::Equal => ordering_entry.start_seq.cmp(&seq),\n                }\n            })\n            .unwrap_or_else(|i| i);\n\n        q.insert(idx, key);\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn schedule_callback(\n        &self,\n        callback: OneshotTimerCallback,\n        duration: Duration,\n        source: TimerSource,\n    ) -> OneshotTimerHandle {\n        let new_handle = self.next_timer_handle.get();\n        self.next_timer_handle\n            .set(OneshotTimerHandle(new_handle.0 + 1));\n\n        let timer = OneshotTimer {\n            handle: new_handle,\n            source,\n            callback,\n            scheduled_for: self.base_time() + duration,\n        };\n\n        // https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#run-steps-after-a-timeout\n        // Step 4.2: maintain per-orderingIdentifier order by milliseconds (and start order for ties).\n        if let OneshotTimerCallback::RunStepsAfterTimeout {\n            ordering_id,\n            milliseconds,\n            ..\n        } = &timer.callback\n        {\n            self.runsteps_enqueue_sorted(ordering_id, new_handle, *milliseconds);\n        }\n\n        {\n            let mut timers = self.timers.borrow_mut();\n            let insertion_index = timers.binary_search(&timer).err().unwrap();\n            timers.insert(insertion_index, timer);\n        }\n\n        if self.is_next_timer(new_handle) {\n            self.schedule_timer_call();\n        }\n\n        new_handle\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn unschedule_callback(&self, handle: OneshotTimerHandle) {\n        let was_next = self.is_next_timer(handle);\n\n        self.timers.borrow_mut().retain(|t| t.handle != handle);\n\n        if was_next {\n            self.invalidate_expected_event_id();\n            self.schedule_timer_call();\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn is_next_timer(&self, handle: OneshotTimerHandle) -> bool {\n        match self.timers.borrow().back() {\n            None => false,\n            Some(max_timer) => max_timer.handle == handle,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#timer-initialisation-steps>\n    pub(crate) fn fire_timer(&self, id: TimerEventId, global: &GlobalScope, can_gc: CanGc) {\n        // Step 9.2. If id does not exist in global's map of setTimeout and setInterval IDs, then abort these steps.\n        let expected_id = self.expected_event_id.get();\n        if expected_id != id {\n            debug!(\n                \"ignoring timer fire event {:?} (expected {:?})\",\n                id, expected_id\n            );\n            return;\n        }\n\n        assert!(self.suspended_since.get().is_none());\n\n        let base_time = self.base_time();\n\n        // Since the event id was the expected one, at least one timer should be due.\n        if base_time < self.timers.borrow().back().unwrap().scheduled_for {\n            warn!(\"Unexpected timing!\");\n            return;\n        }\n\n        // select timers to run to prevent firing timers\n        // that were installed during fire of another timer\n        let mut timers_to_run = Vec::new();\n\n        loop {\n            let mut timers = self.timers.borrow_mut();\n\n            if timers.is_empty() || timers.back().unwrap().scheduled_for > base_time {\n                break;\n            }\n\n            timers_to_run.push(timers.pop_back().unwrap());\n        }\n\n        for timer in timers_to_run {\n            // Since timers can be coalesced together inside a task,\n            // this loop can keep running, including after an interrupt of the JS,\n            // and prevent a clean-shutdown of a JS-running thread.\n            // This check prevents such a situation.\n            if !global.can_continue_running() {\n                return;\n            }\n            match &timer.callback {\n                // TODO: https://github.com/servo/servo/issues/40060\n                OneshotTimerCallback::RunStepsAfterTimeout { ordering_id, .. } => {\n                    // Step 4.2 Wait until any invocations of this algorithm that had the same global and orderingIdentifier,\n                    // that started before this one, and whose milliseconds is less than or equal to this one's, have completed.\n                    let head_handle_opt = {\n                        let queues_ref = self.runsteps_queues.borrow();\n                        queues_ref\n                            .get(ordering_id)\n                            .and_then(|v| v.first().map(|t| t.handle))\n                    };\n                    let is_head = head_handle_opt.is_none_or(|head| head == timer.handle);\n\n                    if !is_head {\n                        // TODO: this re queuing would go away when we revisit timers implementation.\n                        let rein = OneshotTimer {\n                            handle: timer.handle,\n                            source: timer.source,\n                            callback: timer.callback,\n                            scheduled_for: self.base_time(),\n                        };\n                        let mut timers = self.timers.borrow_mut();\n                        let idx = timers.binary_search(&rein).err().unwrap();\n                        timers.insert(idx, rein);\n                        continue;\n                    }\n\n                    let (timer_key, ordering_id_owned, completion) = match timer.callback {\n                        OneshotTimerCallback::RunStepsAfterTimeout {\n                            timer_key,\n                            ordering_id,\n                            milliseconds: _,\n                            completion,\n                        } => (timer_key, ordering_id, completion),\n                        _ => unreachable!(),\n                    };\n\n                    // Step 4.3 Optionally, wait a further implementation-defined length of time.\n                    // (No additional delay applied.)\n\n                    // Step 4.4 Perform completionSteps.\n                    (completion)(global, can_gc);\n\n                    // Step 4.5 Remove global's map of active timers[timerKey].\n                    self.map_of_active_timers.borrow_mut().remove(&timer_key);\n\n                    {\n                        let mut queues_mut = self.runsteps_queues.borrow_mut();\n                        if let Some(q) = queues_mut.get_mut(&ordering_id_owned) {\n                            if !q.is_empty() {\n                                q.remove(0);\n                            }\n                            if q.is_empty() {\n                                queues_mut.remove(&ordering_id_owned);\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                },\n                _ => {\n                    let cb = timer.callback;\n                    cb.invoke(global, &self.js_timers, can_gc);\n                },\n            }\n        }\n\n        self.schedule_timer_call();\n    }\n\n    fn base_time(&self) -> Instant {\n        let offset = self.suspension_offset.get();\n        match self.suspended_since.get() {\n            Some(suspend_time) => suspend_time - offset,\n            None => Instant::now() - offset,\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn slow_down(&self) {\n        let min_duration_ms = pref!(js_timers_minimum_duration) as u64;\n        self.js_timers\n            .set_min_duration(Duration::from_millis(min_duration_ms));\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn speed_up(&self) {\n        self.js_timers.remove_min_duration();\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn suspend(&self) {\n        // Suspend is idempotent: do nothing if the timers are already suspended.\n        if self.suspended_since.get().is_some() {\n            return warn!(\"Suspending an already suspended timer.\");\n        }\n\n        debug!(\"Suspending timers.\");\n        self.suspended_since.set(Some(Instant::now()));\n        self.invalidate_expected_event_id();\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn resume(&self) {\n        // Resume is idempotent: do nothing if the timers are already resumed.\n        let additional_offset = match self.suspended_since.get() {\n            Some(suspended_since) => Instant::now() - suspended_since,\n            None => return warn!(\"Resuming an already resumed timer.\"),\n        };\n\n        debug!(\"Resuming timers.\");\n        self.suspension_offset\n            .set(self.suspension_offset.get() + additional_offset);\n        self.suspended_since.set(None);\n\n        self.schedule_timer_call();\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#timer-initialisation-steps>\n    fn schedule_timer_call(&self) {\n        if self.suspended_since.get().is_some() {\n            // The timer will be scheduled when the pipeline is fully activated.\n            return;\n        }\n\n        let timers = self.timers.borrow();\n        let Some(timer) = timers.back() else {\n            return;\n        };\n\n        let expected_event_id = self.invalidate_expected_event_id();\n        // Step 12. Let completionStep be an algorithm step which queues a global\n        // task on the timer task source given global to run task.\n        let callback = TimerListener {\n            context: Trusted::new(&*self.global_scope),\n            task_source: self\n                .global_scope\n                .task_manager()\n                .timer_task_source()\n                .to_sendable(),\n            source: timer.source,\n            id: expected_event_id,\n        }\n        .into_callback();\n\n        let event_request = TimerEventRequest {\n            callback,\n            duration: timer.scheduled_for - self.base_time(),\n        };\n\n        self.global_scope.schedule_timer(event_request);\n    }\n\n    fn invalidate_expected_event_id(&self) -> TimerEventId {\n        let TimerEventId(currently_expected) = self.expected_event_id.get();\n        let next_id = TimerEventId(currently_expected + 1);\n        debug!(\n            \"invalidating expected timer (was {:?}, now {:?}\",\n            currently_expected, next_id\n        );\n        self.expected_event_id.set(next_id);\n        next_id\n    }\n\n    #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]\n    pub(crate) fn set_timeout_or_interval(\n        &self,\n        global: &GlobalScope,\n        callback: TimerCallback,\n        arguments: Vec<HandleValue>,\n        timeout: Duration,\n        is_interval: IsInterval,\n        source: TimerSource,\n        can_gc: CanGc,\n    ) -> Fallible<i32> {\n        self.js_timers.set_timeout_or_interval(\n            global,\n            callback,\n            arguments,\n            timeout,\n            is_interval,\n            source,\n            can_gc,\n        )\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn clear_timeout_or_interval(&self, global: &GlobalScope, handle: i32) {\n        self.js_timers.clear_timeout_or_interval(global, handle)\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, Eq, Hash, JSTraceable, MallocSizeOf, Ord, PartialEq, PartialOrd)]\npub(crate) struct JsTimerHandle(i32);\n\n#[derive(DenyPublicFields, JSTraceable, MallocSizeOf)]\npub(crate) struct JsTimers {\n    next_timer_handle: Cell<JsTimerHandle>,\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#list-of-active-timers>\n    active_timers: DomRefCell<FxHashMap<JsTimerHandle, JsTimerEntry>>,\n    /// The nesting level of the currently executing timer task or 0.\n    nesting_level: Cell<u32>,\n    /// Used to introduce a minimum delay in event intervals\n    min_duration: Cell<Option<Duration>>,\n}\n\n#[derive(JSTraceable, MallocSizeOf)]\nstruct JsTimerEntry {\n    oneshot_handle: OneshotTimerHandle,\n}\n\n// Holder for the various JS values associated with setTimeout\n// (ie. function value to invoke and all arguments to pass\n//      to the function when calling it)\n// TODO: Handle rooting during invocation when movable GC is turned on\n#[derive(JSTraceable, MallocSizeOf)]\npub(crate) struct JsTimerTask {\n    handle: JsTimerHandle,\n    #[no_trace]\n    source: TimerSource,\n    callback: InternalTimerCallback,\n    is_interval: IsInterval,\n    nesting_level: u32,\n    duration: Duration,\n    is_user_interacting: bool,\n}\n\n// Enum allowing more descriptive values for the is_interval field\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, JSTraceable, MallocSizeOf, PartialEq)]\npub(crate) enum IsInterval {\n    Interval,\n    NonInterval,\n}\n\npub(crate) enum TimerCallback {\n    StringTimerCallback(TrustedScriptOrString),\n    FunctionTimerCallback(Rc<Function>),\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, JSTraceable, MallocSizeOf)]\n#[cfg_attr(crown, expect(crown::unrooted_must_root))]\nenum InternalTimerCallback {\n    StringTimerCallback(DOMString),\n    FunctionTimerCallback(\n        #[conditional_malloc_size_of] Rc<Function>,\n        #[ignore_malloc_size_of = \"mozjs\"] Rc<Box<[Heap<JSVal>]>>,\n    ),\n}\n\nimpl Default for JsTimers {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        JsTimers {\n            next_timer_handle: Cell::new(JsTimerHandle(1)),\n            active_timers: DomRefCell::new(FxHashMap::default()),\n            nesting_level: Cell::new(0),\n            min_duration: Cell::new(None),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl JsTimers {\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#timer-initialisation-steps>\n    #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]\n    #[cfg_attr(crown, expect(crown::unrooted_must_root))]\n    pub(crate) fn set_timeout_or_interval(\n        &self,\n        global: &GlobalScope,\n        callback: TimerCallback,\n        arguments: Vec<HandleValue>,\n        timeout: Duration,\n        is_interval: IsInterval,\n        source: TimerSource,\n        can_gc: CanGc,\n    ) -> Fallible<i32> {\n        let callback = match callback {\n            TimerCallback::StringTimerCallback(trusted_script_or_string) => {\n                // Step 9.6.1.1. Let globalName be \"Window\" if global is a Window object; \"WorkerGlobalScope\" otherwise.\n                let global_name = if global.is::<Window>() {\n                    \"Window\"\n                } else {\n                    \"WorkerGlobalScope\"\n                };\n                // Step 9.6.1.2. Let methodName be \"setInterval\" if repeat is true; \"setTimeout\" otherwise.\n                let method_name = if is_interval == IsInterval::Interval {\n                    \"setInterval\"\n                } else {\n                    \"setTimeout\"\n                };\n                // Step 9.6.1.3. Let sink be a concatenation of globalName, U+0020 SPACE, and methodName.\n                let sink = format!(\"{} {}\", global_name, method_name);\n                // Step 9.6.1.4. Set handler to the result of invoking the\n                // Get Trusted Type compliant string algorithm with TrustedScript, global, handler, sink, and \"script\".\n                let code_str = TrustedScript::get_trusted_script_compliant_string(\n                    global,\n                    trusted_script_or_string,\n                    &sink,\n                    can_gc,\n                )?;\n                // Step 9.6.3. Perform EnsureCSPDoesNotBlockStringCompilation(realm, « », handler, handler, timer, « », handler).\n                // If this throws an exception, catch it, report it for global, and abort these steps.\n                if global\n                    .get_csp_list()\n                    .is_js_evaluation_allowed(global, &code_str.str())\n                {\n                    // Step 9.6.2. Assert: handler is a string.\n                    InternalTimerCallback::StringTimerCallback(code_str)\n                } else {\n                    return Ok(0);\n                }\n            },\n            TimerCallback::FunctionTimerCallback(function) => {\n                // This is a bit complicated, but this ensures that the vector's\n                // buffer isn't reallocated (and moved) after setting the Heap values\n                let mut args = Vec::with_capacity(arguments.len());\n                for _ in 0..arguments.len() {\n                    args.push(Heap::default());\n                }\n                for (i, item) in arguments.iter().enumerate() {\n                    args.get_mut(i).unwrap().set(item.get());\n                }\n                // Step 9.5. If handler is a Function, then invoke handler given arguments and \"report\",\n                // and with callback this value set to thisArg.\n                InternalTimerCallback::FunctionTimerCallback(\n                    function,\n                    Rc::new(args.into_boxed_slice()),\n                )\n            },\n        };\n\n        // Step 2. If previousId was given, let id be previousId; otherwise,\n        // let id be an implementation-defined integer that is greater than zero\n        // and does not already exist in global's map of setTimeout and setInterval IDs.\n        let JsTimerHandle(new_handle) = self.next_timer_handle.get();\n        self.next_timer_handle.set(JsTimerHandle(new_handle + 1));\n\n        // Step 3. If the surrounding agent's event loop's currently running task\n        // is a task that was created by this algorithm, then let nesting level\n        // be the task's timer nesting level. Otherwise, let nesting level be 0.\n        let mut task = JsTimerTask {\n            handle: JsTimerHandle(new_handle),\n            source,\n            callback,\n            is_interval,\n            is_user_interacting: ScriptThread::is_user_interacting(),\n            nesting_level: 0,\n            duration: Duration::ZERO,\n        };\n\n        // Step 4. If timeout is less than 0, then set timeout to 0.\n        task.duration = timeout.max(Duration::ZERO);\n\n        self.initialize_and_schedule(global, task);\n\n        // Step 15. Return id.\n        Ok(new_handle)\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn clear_timeout_or_interval(&self, global: &GlobalScope, handle: i32) {\n        let mut active_timers = self.active_timers.borrow_mut();\n\n        if let Some(entry) = active_timers.remove(&JsTimerHandle(handle)) {\n            global.unschedule_callback(entry.oneshot_handle);\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn set_min_duration(&self, duration: Duration) {\n        self.min_duration.set(Some(duration));\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn remove_min_duration(&self) {\n        self.min_duration.set(None);\n    }\n\n    // see step 13 of https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#timer-initialisation-steps\n    fn user_agent_pad(&self, current_duration: Duration) -> Duration {\n        match self.min_duration.get() {\n            Some(min_duration) => min_duration.max(current_duration),\n            None => current_duration,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#timer-initialisation-steps>\n    fn initialize_and_schedule(&self, global: &GlobalScope, mut task: JsTimerTask) {\n        let handle = task.handle;\n        let mut active_timers = self.active_timers.borrow_mut();\n\n        // Step 3. If the surrounding agent's event loop's currently running task\n        // is a task that was created by this algorithm, then let nesting level be\n        // the task's timer nesting level. Otherwise, let nesting level be 0.\n        let nesting_level = self.nesting_level.get();\n\n        let duration = self.user_agent_pad(clamp_duration(nesting_level, task.duration));\n        // Step 10. Increment nesting level by one.\n        // Step 11. Set task's timer nesting level to nesting level.\n        task.nesting_level = nesting_level + 1;\n\n        // Step 13. Set uniqueHandle to the result of running steps after a timeout given global,\n        // \"setTimeout/setInterval\", timeout, and completionStep.\n        let callback = OneshotTimerCallback::JsTimer(task);\n        let oneshot_handle = global.schedule_callback(callback, duration);\n\n        // Step 14. Set global's map of setTimeout and setInterval IDs[id] to uniqueHandle.\n        let entry = active_timers\n            .entry(handle)\n            .or_insert(JsTimerEntry { oneshot_handle });\n        entry.oneshot_handle = oneshot_handle;\n    }\n}\n\n/// Step 5 of <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#timer-initialisation-steps>\nfn clamp_duration(nesting_level: u32, unclamped: Duration) -> Duration {\n    // Step 5. If nesting level is greater than 5, and timeout is less than 4, then set timeout to 4.\n    let lower_bound_ms = if nesting_level > 5 { 4 } else { 0 };\n    let lower_bound = Duration::from_millis(lower_bound_ms);\n    lower_bound.max(unclamped)\n}\n\nimpl JsTimerTask {\n    // see https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#timer-initialisation-steps\n    pub(crate) fn invoke<T: DomObject>(self, this: &T, timers: &JsTimers, can_gc: CanGc) {\n        // step 9.2 can be ignored, because we proactively prevent execution\n        // of this task when its scheduled execution is canceled.\n\n        // prep for step ? in nested set_timeout_or_interval calls\n        timers.nesting_level.set(self.nesting_level);\n\n        let _guard = ScriptThread::user_interacting_guard();\n        match self.callback {\n            InternalTimerCallback::StringTimerCallback(ref code_str) => {\n                // Step 6.4. Let settings object be global's relevant settings object.\n                // Step 6. Let realm be global's relevant realm.\n                let global = this.global();\n                // TODO Step 7. Let initiating script be the active script.\n\n                // Step 9.6.5. Let fetch options be the default script fetch options.\n                let fetch_options = ScriptFetchOptions::default_classic_script(&global);\n\n                // Step 9.6.6. Let base URL be settings object's API base URL.\n                let base_url = global.api_base_url();\n\n                // TODO Step 9.6.7. If initiating script is not null, then:\n                // Step 9.6.7.1. Set fetch options to a script fetch options whose cryptographic nonce\n                // is initiating script's fetch options's cryptographic nonce,\n                // integrity metadata is the empty string, parser metadata is \"not-parser-inserted\",\n                // credentials mode is initiating script's fetch options's credentials mode,\n                // referrer policy is initiating script's fetch options's referrer policy,\n                // and fetch priority is \"auto\".\n                // Step 9.6.7.2. Set base URL to initiating script's base URL.\n\n                // Step 9.6.8. Let script be the result of creating a classic script given handler,\n                // settings object, base URL, and fetch options.\n                let script = global.create_a_classic_script(\n                    (*code_str.str()).into(),\n                    base_url,\n                    fetch_options,\n                    ErrorReporting::Unmuted,\n                    Some(IntroductionType::DOM_TIMER),\n                    1,\n                    false,\n                );\n\n                // Step 9.6.9. Run the classic script script.\n                _ = global.run_a_classic_script(script, RethrowErrors::No, can_gc);\n            },\n            // Step 9.5. If handler is a Function, then invoke handler given arguments and\n            // \"report\", and with callback this value set to thisArg.\n            InternalTimerCallback::FunctionTimerCallback(ref function, ref arguments) => {\n                let arguments = self.collect_heap_args(arguments);\n                rooted!(in(*GlobalScope::get_cx()) let mut value: JSVal);\n                let _ = function.Call_(this, arguments, value.handle_mut(), Report, can_gc);\n            },\n        };\n\n        // reset nesting level (see above)\n        timers.nesting_level.set(0);\n\n        // Step 9.9. If repeat is true, then perform the timer initialization steps again,\n        // given global, handler, timeout, arguments, true, and id.\n        //\n        // Since we choose proactively prevent execution (see 4.1 above), we must only\n        // reschedule repeating timers when they were not canceled as part of step 4.2.\n        if self.is_interval == IsInterval::Interval &&\n            timers.active_timers.borrow().contains_key(&self.handle)\n        {\n            timers.initialize_and_schedule(&this.global(), self);\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn collect_heap_args<'b>(&self, args: &'b [Heap<JSVal>]) -> Vec<HandleValue<'b>> {\n        args.iter().map(|arg| arg.as_handle_value()).collect()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Describes the source that requested the [`TimerEvent`].\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, Deserialize, MallocSizeOf, Serialize)]\npub enum TimerSource {\n    /// The event was requested from a window (`ScriptThread`).\n    FromWindow(PipelineId),\n    /// The event was requested from a worker (`DedicatedGlobalWorkerScope`).\n    FromWorker,\n}\n\n/// The id to be used for a [`TimerEvent`] is defined by the corresponding [`TimerEventRequest`].\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, Deserialize, Eq, MallocSizeOf, PartialEq, Serialize)]\npub struct TimerEventId(pub u32);\n\n/// A notification that a timer has fired. [`TimerSource`] must be `FromWindow` when\n/// dispatched to `ScriptThread` and must be `FromWorker` when dispatched to a\n/// `DedicatedGlobalWorkerScope`\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, Deserialize, Serialize)]\npub struct TimerEvent(pub TimerSource, pub TimerEventId);\n\n/// A wrapper between timer events coming in over IPC, and the event-loop.\n#[derive(Clone)]\nstruct TimerListener {\n    task_source: SendableTaskSource,\n    context: Trusted<GlobalScope>,\n    source: TimerSource,\n    id: TimerEventId,\n}\n\nimpl TimerListener {\n    /// Handle a timer-event coming from the [`timers::TimerScheduler`]\n    /// by queuing the appropriate task on the relevant event-loop.\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#timer-initialisation-steps>\n    fn handle(&self, event: TimerEvent) {\n        let context = self.context.clone();\n        // Step 9. Let task be a task that runs the following substeps:\n        self.task_source.queue(task!(timer_event: move || {\n                let global = context.root();\n                let TimerEvent(source, id) = event;\n                match source {\n                    TimerSource::FromWorker => {\n                        global.downcast::<WorkerGlobalScope>().expect(\"Window timer delivered to worker\");\n                    },\n                    TimerSource::FromWindow(pipeline) => {\n                        assert_eq!(pipeline, global.pipeline_id());\n                        global.downcast::<Window>().expect(\"Worker timer delivered to window\");\n                    },\n                };\n                global.fire_timer(id, CanGc::note());\n            })\n        );\n    }\n\n    fn into_callback(self) -> BoxedTimerCallback {\n        let timer_event = TimerEvent(self.source, self.id);\n        Box::new(move || self.handle(timer_event))\n    }\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":30,"oldLines":7,"newStart":30,"newLines":7,"lines":[" use crate::dom::bindings::root::{AsHandleValue, Dom};"," use crate::dom::bindings::str::DOMString;"," use crate::dom::csp::CspReporting;","-use crate::dom::document::RefreshRedirectDue;","+use crate::dom::document::{RefreshRedirectDue, ScrollendCallback};"," use crate::dom::eventsource::EventSourceTimeoutCallback;"," use crate::dom::global_scope_script_execution::{ErrorReporting, RethrowErrors};"," use crate::dom::globalscope::GlobalScope;"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

RunStepsDeadline>;\n\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, Eq, Hash, JSTraceable, MallocSizeOf, Ord, PartialEq, PartialOrd)]\npub(crate) struct OneshotTimerHandle(i32);\n\n#[derive(DenyPublicFields, JSTraceable, MallocSizeOf)]\n#[cfg_attr(crown, crown::unrooted_must_root_lint::must_root)]\npub(crate) struct OneshotTimers {\n    global_scope: Dom<GlobalScope>,\n    js_timers: JsTimers,\n
